_comment: Single source of truth for the guided-visualization narration. The app (sprints/sprint-flow.tsx) builds the on-screen prompts from this, and scripts/generate-narration.mjs reads the same text to render /public/audio/{id}.mp3 via ElevenLabs. Editing a line's text means regenerating that clip (npm run narration). holdMs is the silent visualization pause AFTER the line is read aloud.

sprintFuture.endText: Now open your eyes and fill out these boxes.

steps.text: Close your eyes. It is the last day of this 10-to-14 day sprint. This is not a fantasy. This is your realistic best-case outcome if you did the work every day.
Look back over the push: what did you do on the hard days that you would normally have skipped?
What is the one task on this board that mattered most — and how does it feel now that it is done?
Who would notice the change this sprint made in you, and what exactly would they see?
Freeze frame: what single moment from these two weeks best captures the win?

sprintObstacle.endText: Now open your eyes and write the obstacle in one sentence.

steps.text: Now contrast that finish with reality: what inside you is most likely to derail this sprint? Close your eyes and picture the exact moment it happens vividly.
The thought that will tempt you to skip a task is...
The emotion that comes with it is...
The behavior it usually triggers is...
